## (a) Implementation

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id
{
  if x.width >= PAD then x
  else Id(x.kind, x.value, PAD)
}
```

## (b) Lemma specification

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).kind == x.kind          // kind never changes
  ensures Canonicalize(x).value == x.value        // numeric value never changes
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width == if x.width >= PAD then x.width else PAD
                                                  // width = max(old width, PAD), i.e. min 4 digits
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= PAD            // result is at least canonical width
  ensures Wellformed(Canonicalize(x))             // result is still wellformed
  ensures x.width >= PAD ==> Canonicalize(x) == x // already-canonical is a no-op
  ensures Canonicalize(Canonicalize(x)) == Canonicalize(x) // idempotent
{ }
```

### Notes on completeness

The core contract is captured by the first three `ensures` clauses:

- **kind** and **value** are preserved exactly (so the id refers to the same entity).
- **width** becomes `max(x.width, PAD)`: an id at 4+ digits is unchanged, otherwise it is padded up to the minimum of 4. Because `Wellformed(x)` holds (`x.width >= NumDigits(x.value)`), this width is exactly "left-zero-pad the value to a minimum of 4 digits."

The remaining clauses are logical consequences of the above, included to make the audited contract explicit:

- result width `>= PAD`,
- result is `Wellformed`,
- the **no-op** property for already-canonical ids,
- **idempotence** of canonicalization.
